name: Muffins Basic & Variations
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
2. Grease 12 2‑1/2-inch muffin cups.
3. In a large bowl, sift together the flour, baking powder, sugar, and salt.
4. In a separate bowl, beat the egg with a fork. Add milk and oil; stir until combined.
5. Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and mix only until moistened—batter will be lumpy.
6. Spoon batter into prepared muffin cups, filling each about halfway to two-thirds full.
7. Bake for 15–20 minutes, or until golden brown. Remove from pan and serve hot with butter, jam, or marmalade.
Variations:
GINGER MUFFINS: Add finely diced candied ginger (½ cup) to the flour mixture before adding liquid.
BANANA PECAN MUFFINS: Use only ½ cup milk. Add chopped pecans (½ cup), ground nutmeg (¼ teaspoon), and mashed banana (1 cup) with the egg, oil, and milk.
BLUEBERRY MUFFINS: Toss washed blueberries (fresh or frozen, 1 cup) with the flour mixture before adding liquid.
ORANGE MUFFINS: Top each muffin with a peel of orange and sprinkle lightly with sugar.
CHEESE MUFFINS: Fold grated sharp yellow cheese (½ cup) into the batter during the final mixing.
SURPRISE MUFFINS: Add a dollop of jelly in the center of each muffin before completing the filling.
COCONUT MUFFINS: Mix shredded coconut (1 cup) into the batter just before pouring it into the cups.
CHIVE MUFFINS: Fold chopped chives (¼ cup) into the batter during the last few strokes.
